deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, when students successfully articulate the means/side-effect distinction or refine their rule after earlier confusion, the tutor consistently moves forward to the next challenge without prompting reflection on what changed in the student's thinking or why the improved formulation worked better.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts, the tutor demonstrates strong Socratic practice: it never gives away the answer, consistently prompts students to classify cases themselves, and builds progressively from the basic consequentialist rule toward the means/side-effect distinction. Responses are concise and well-anchored to the assignment. The one consistent gap is the absence of reflective prompts after student breakthroughs—when students successfully articulate the key distinction, the tutor moves immediately to the next challenge rather than asking what changed in their thinking. No other rubric violations were found.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: The tutor never provides submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and never states the key conclusion before the student derives it. All three transcripts show the student arriving at the means/side-effect distinction through their own reasoning, guided by targeted questions. Full marks. 1.2: The tutor consistently acknowledges correct student steps (e.g., 'That's a solid start,' 'Good—that's a real philosophical distinction') and builds on them rather than restarting. Diagnostic questioning is used appropriately before redirecting. Full marks. 1.3: The tutor explains its tutoring rationale implicitly through its method (e.g., Transcript 2 Turn 9: 'Don't outsource the recall') and provides specific process feedback (e.g., 'stop saying my intuition and ask what value your rule is protecting'). However, in all three transcripts, when students successfully improve their formulation after earlier uncertainty, the tutor never pauses to ask what changed or why the new approach worked—it simply advances to the next question. This omission triggers 1.3.C.a for 2 points. 2.1–2.2: No repetition without progress, no off-topic drift, and no misunderstood assignment framing goes uncorrected. 3.1–3.2: Responses are consistently bite-sized, precise, and supportive without implying formal grading.
